note: Source of truth for architectural decisions. ARCHITECTURE.md is generated from this map. Add concerns here when ADRs are accepted.

substrate-workflow-boundary.summary: Platform owns all data. Workflows configure how agents interact with it. Platform DDL is stable; workflow fields live in metadata JSON blobs.

workflow-schema-immutability.summary: Workflow schema (doc kinds, field shapes) is immutable per project. Orchestration (profiles, routing, hooks) is hot-swappable above it.

platform-doc-types.summary: Note and ADR are platform-level doc types. Workflows register additional kinds via workflow.toml. Schema validation is workflow-layer, not platform-layer.

architecture-map.summary: This file is the source of truth. ARCHITECTURE.md is generated output. ADRs are the rich content; this map is the index.

extension-model.summary: Platform exposes hook points in workflow.toml. Extensions register MCP tools. Environment-specific scripts live in opt-in skills, not platform code.

free-cloud-split.summary: Local = free. Cloud/team = paid. Paywall is the trust boundary: when user data flows through Ship Cloud, that is the paid tier.
